POS OF SUPPLEMENTAL REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE; SUPPLEMENTAL DECLARATOIN OF DANIELLE OCHS-TILLOTSON FILED BY DEFENDANT RYN, GORAN , AN INDIVIDUAL

LAW AND MOTION 302, ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE RE PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION AND TEMPORARY RESTRAINING ORDER. PLAINTIFFS APPLICATION FOR PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION IS ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: GRANTED. PLAINTIFFS CLAIM IS NOT BARRED BY THE DOCTRINE OF COLLATERAL ESTOPPEL. A PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION IS NOT A FINAL DECISION ON THE MERITS. HUNTINGDON LIFE SCI., INC. V. STOP HUNTINGDON ANIMAL CRUELTY 129 CAL.APP.4TH 1228. PLAINTIFFS HAVE DEMONSTRATED THEY ARE LIKELY TO PREVAIL ON THE MERITS OF THEIR CLAIMS BASED UPON DEFENDANT'S REPUDIATON OF THE NONSOLICITATION COVENANT IN THE STOCK PURCHASE AGREEMENT. ALLIANT INS. SERV. INC. V. GADDY 159 CAL.APP.4TH 1292. THERE IS NO INDICATION FROM THE TEXT OF THE EMPLOYMENT AGREEMENT THAT IT IS INTENDED TO SUPERCEDE THE STOCK PURCHASE AGREEMENT. ALSO, SEE VACCO INDUS. V. VANDENBERG 5 CAL.APP.4TH 34. THE CONSIDERATION OF THE RELATIVE HARDSHIPS FAVORS GRANTING INJUNCTIVE RELIEF. THE PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION IS NARROWLY TAILORED, ONLY PREVENTING DEFENDANT FROM DOING BUSINESS WITH 4 OF THE CLIENTS HE SERVICED WHEN HE SOLD HIS BUSINESS TO PLAINTIFFS. DEFENDANT OVER HIS LONG CAREER HAS BUILT UP NUMEROUS CONTACTS IN THE CONSTRUCTION INDUSTRY AND WILL NOT SUFFER ANY APPRECIABLE HARDSHIP IF HE IS BARRED FROM SERVICING THOSE 4 CLIENTS FOR 3 YEARS. PLAINTIFFS ON THE OTHER HAND, WILL SUFFER SUBSTANTIAL INJURY AND HARM THROUGH LOSS OF BUSINESS AND GOODWILL. THE APPLICATION IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFF TO POST BOND IN THE AMOUNT OF $150,000. THE BOND IS TO BE POSTED ONE WEEK FROM TODAY. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER. (302/CWW) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOO

Order To Show Cause Re Preliminary Injunction And Temporary Restraining Order
Off Calendar Jan-30-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Order To Show Cause Re Preliminary Injunction And Temporary Restraining Order. Appearances: Michael Kump, Esq., of Kinsella Weitzman Iser Kump & Aldisert LLP,(310) 566-9855, appearing for plaintiffs USI Holdings Corporation and USI Insurance Services of Northern California, Inc. and moving parties; Danielle Ochs-Tillotson, Esq., of Ogletree Deakins, Nash, Smoak & Stewart, P.C., (415) 536-3429, appearing for Defendant Gorn Ryn and opposing party.
PLAINTIFFS APPLICATION FOR PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION is Argued and The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: GRANTED. PLAINTIFFS CLAIM IS NOT BARRED BY THE DOCTRINE OF COLLATERAL ESTOPPEL. A PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION IS NOT A FINAL DECISION ON THE MERITS. HUNTINGDON LIFE SCI., INC. V. STOP HUNTINGDON ANIMAL CRUELTY 129 CAL.APP.4TH 1228. PLAINTIFFS HAVE DEMONSTRATED THEY ARE LIKELY TO PREVAIL ON THE MERITS OF THEIR CLAIMS BASED UPON DEFENDANT'S REPUDIATON OF THE NONSOLICITATION COVENANT IN THE STOCK PURCHASE AGREEMENT. ALLIANT INS. SERV. INC. V. GADDY 159 CAL.APP.4TH 1292. THERE IS NO INDICATION FROM THE TEXT OF THE EMPLOYMENT AGREEMENT THAT IT IS INTENDED TO SUPERCEDE THE STOCK PURCHASE AGREEMENT. ALSO, SEE VACCO INDUS. V. VANDENBERG 5 CAL.APP.4TH 34. THE CONSIDERATION OF THE RELATIVE HARDSHIPS FAVORS GRANTING INJUNCTIVE RELIEF.
THE PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION IS NARROWLY TAILORED, ONLY PREVENTING DEFENDANT FROM DOING BUSINESS WITH 4 OF THE CLIENTS HE SERVICED WHEN HE SOLD HIS BUSINESS TO PLAINTIFFS. DEFENDANT OVER HIS LONG CAREER HAS BUILT UP NUMEROUS CONTACTS IN THE CONSTRUCTION INDUSTRY AND WILL NOT SUFFER ANY APPRECIABLE HARDSHIP IF HE IS BARRED FROM SERVICING THOSE 4 CLIENTS FOR 3 YEARS. PLAINTIFFS ON THE OTHER HAND, WILL SUFFER SUBSTANTIAL INJURY AND HARM THROUGH LOSS OF BUSINESS AND GOODWILL. THE APPLICATION IS GRANTED. Plaintiff to post bond in the amount of $150,000. The bond is to be posted one week from today. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order. (302/CWW/JW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard, Reporter: Ke
